channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: China-Linked SprySOCKS Backdoor Expands to Windows with Driver-Based Stealth
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged two previously undocumented Windows variants of what was believed to be a Linux-only backdoor called SprySOCKS.
"The Windows variants discovered are internally ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cisco Releases Security Updates for Actively Exploited SD-WAN Manager Flaw
item.description:
Cisco has released security updates for a medium-severity security flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Manager that has come under active exploitation in the wild.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20262, c...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA Flags LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin Flaw Exploited for Root Privilege Escalation
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a security flaw impacting LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, requiring Federal Civ...

item.title: Scoop: FulcrumSec Leaks Novo Nordisk Data After $25M Demand Goes Unpaid (2)
item.description: Danish pharma giant Novo Nordisk disclosed a cybersecurity incident last week, and although the firm’s name may not be familiar to everyone, they are a major producer of insulin and semaglutide. Semag...

item.title: Chinese Hackers Abused Google Workspace Rules to Steal Research and Defense Emails
item.description:
A China-linked espionage group hid inside North American medical, academic, and military research networks for more than a year, quietly stealing sensitive research and defense email.
The way in was ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: HTTP/2 Bomb Attacks Put Telcos, Healthcare Orgs at Risk
item.description: The denial-of-service (DoS) exploit takes advantage of two features in HTTP/2 that were designed to save Internet bandwith, not power massive amplification attacks....

item.title: Copilot 'SearchLeak' Attack Allows 1-Click Data Theft

item.title: China-Nexus Actor Spies on US Researchers Undetected for a Year
